'''Niigata Gokoku Shrine''' (新潟縣護國神社, Niigata-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}}) located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Niigata|en|Chūō-ku, Niigata|WD=Q711787|lt=Chūō-ku, Niigata}} in {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|WD=Q179691|lt=Niigata, Niigata}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}}.

The shrine enshrines war dead from Niigata Prefecture from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}} to the {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=Second World War}}, and currently houses 79,729 deities.

== History ==
The {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}, which started with the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}} and eventually spread nationwide, also turned Niigata City into a fierce battlefield. Many lives were lost on both sides: the Western Army (new government forces from {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain}}, {{ill|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|ja|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshū Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q876267}}, etc.) and the Eastern Army ({{ill|Yonezawa Domain|en|Yonezawa Domain|WD=Q1037948|lt=Yonezawa Domain}}, {{ill|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|ja|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389944}}, {{ill|Shōnai Domain|en|Shōnai Domain|WD=Q847303|lt=Shōnai Domain}}). In October 1868 (Meiji 1), a monument for the Western Army war dead was established in Tokiwagaoka (former site of {{ill|Niigata University|en|Niigata University|WD=Q267111|lt=Niigata University}} headquarters), and the shrine was built to enshrine 416 deities of the Boshin War's government forces as Niigata Shōkonsha. In 1875 (Meiji 8), it was renamed to Kankai Shōkonsha{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In July 1941 (Shōwa 16), it was renamed to Gokoku Shrine by Ministry of Home Affairs ordinance. There was intense competition to host the shrine among Niigata City, {{ill|Shibata, Niigata|en|Shibata, Niigata|WD=Q601950|lt=Shibata, Niigata}}, and {{ill|Takada, Niigata|ja|高田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada, Niigata|1=Takada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672106}}. In 1941 (Shōwa 16), Niigata City was chosen{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

From June 1942 (Shōwa 17), relocation to Nishifunamimachi began, and by May 1945 (Shōwa 20), the relocation was completed with the mobilization of 25,000 people{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In 1985 (Shōwa 60), 92 sets of remains believed to be from the Eastern Army war dead were discovered at the former Niigata University headquarters site, and they were reburied in the "Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery" within the Gokoku Shrine grounds. In 1987 (Shōwa 62), the "Boshin War Eastern Army Memorial" was erected.

[[File:Boshin-eki Junnansha-cemetery.jpg|200px|thumb|Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery (Entrance)]]
[[File:Niigatagokokujinjya Syamon.jpg|200px|thumb|Myanmar White Marble Shrine Crest]]

== Grounds and Facilities ==
* Niigata City Sumo Arena (now closed)
* Basho Hall
* Sakaguchi Ango Memorial
* Kita Yoshitaka Monument (Monument of the song "Sunayama")
* Monument for the Victims of Manchurian and Mongolian Pioneers{{sfn|小林朗|2004}}
* Meiji University War Dead Memorial Hall

== Visitor Information ==
; Access
* From "Gokoku Shrine Entrance" bus stop on the {{ill|Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|ja|新潟市の交通#観光循環バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|1=transport in Niigata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16485562}}, a short walk
* About 5 minutes on foot from "Okamoto Shoji" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu {{ill|C2|ja|新潟交通のバス路線一覧#C2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/C2|1=C2|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502828}} Hamaura Line (from Niigata Station)
* Immediately from "Gokoku Shrine" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu C2 Hamaura Line (towards Niigata)
* Parking available

; Nearby
In the vicinity, there are several tourist attractions including aquariums, many schools, and the Japan Sea is close by. The coast near the shrine is selected as one of Japan's "100 best white sand and green pine beaches"{{sfn|林野庁|2019}}.
* {{ill|Marinepia Nihonkai|ja|マリンピア日本海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marinepia Nihonkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟市水族館 マリンピア日本海|1=Niigata City Aquarium Marinepia Nihonkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502898}}

'''Niigata Gokoku Shrine''' (新潟縣護國神社, Niigata-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}}) located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Niigata|en|Chūō-ku, Niigata|WD=Q711787|lt=Chūō-ku, Niigata}} in {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|WD=Q179691|lt=Niigata, Niigata}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}}.

The shrine enshrines war dead from Niigata Prefecture from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}} to the {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=Second World War}}, and currently houses 79,729 deities.

== History ==
The {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}, which started with the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}} and eventually spread nationwide, also turned Niigata City into a fierce battlefield. Many lives were lost on both sides: the Western Army (new government forces from {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain}}, {{ill|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|ja|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshū Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q876267}}, etc.) and the Eastern Army ({{ill|Yonezawa Domain|en|Yonezawa Domain|WD=Q1037948|lt=Yonezawa Domain}}, {{ill|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|ja|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389944}}, {{ill|Shōnai Domain|en|Shōnai Domain|WD=Q847303|lt=Shōnai Domain}}). In October 1868 (Meiji 1), a monument for the Western Army war dead was established in Tokiwagaoka (former site of {{ill|Niigata University|en|Niigata University|WD=Q267111|lt=Niigata University}} headquarters), and the shrine was built to enshrine 416 deities of the Boshin War's government forces as Niigata Shōkonsha. In 1875 (Meiji 8), it was renamed to Kankai Shōkonsha{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In July 1941 (Shōwa 16), it was renamed to Gokoku Shrine by Ministry of Home Affairs ordinance. There was intense competition to host the shrine among Niigata City, {{ill|Shibata, Niigata|en|Shibata, Niigata|WD=Q601950|lt=Shibata, Niigata}}, and {{ill|Takada, Niigata|ja|高田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada, Niigata|1=Takada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672106}}. In 1941 (Shōwa 16), Niigata City was chosen{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

From June 1942 (Shōwa 17), relocation to Nishifunamimachi began, and by May 1945 (Shōwa 20), the relocation was completed with the mobilization of 25,000 people{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In 1985 (Shōwa 60), 92 sets of remains believed to be from the Eastern Army war dead were discovered at the former Niigata University headquarters site, and they were reburied in the "Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery" within the Gokoku Shrine grounds. In 1987 (Shōwa 62), the "Boshin War Eastern Army Memorial" was erected.

[[File:Boshin-eki Junnansha-cemetery.jpg|200px|thumb|Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery (Entrance)]]
[[File:Niigatagokokujinjya Syamon.jpg|200px|thumb|Myanmar White Marble Shrine Crest]]

== Grounds and Facilities ==
* Niigata City Sumo Arena (now closed)
* Basho Hall
* Sakaguchi Ango Memorial
* Kita Yoshitaka Monument (Monument of the song "Sunayama")
* Monument for the Victims of Manchurian and Mongolian Pioneers{{sfn|小林朗|2004}}
* Meiji University War Dead Memorial Hall

== Visitor Information ==
; Access
* From "Gokoku Shrine Entrance" bus stop on the {{ill|Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|ja|新潟市の交通#観光循環バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|1=transport in Niigata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16485562}}, a short walk
* About 5 minutes on foot from "Okamoto Shoji" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu {{ill|C2|ja|新潟交通のバス路線一覧#C2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/C2|1=C2|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502828}} Hamaura Line (from Niigata Station)
* Immediately from "Gokoku Shrine" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu C2 Hamaura Line (towards Niigata)
* Parking available

; Nearby
In the vicinity, there are several tourist attractions including aquariums, many schools, and the Japan Sea is close by. The coast near the shrine is selected as one of Japan's "100 best white sand and green pine beaches"{{sfn|林野庁|2019}}.
* {{ill|Marinepia Nihonkai|ja|マリンピア日本海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marinepia Nihonkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟市水族館 マリンピア日本海|1=Niigata City Aquarium Marinepia Nihonkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502898}}
* {{ill|Nishikaigan Park|ja|西海岸公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikaigan Park|1=West Coast Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629080}}
* {{ill|Japan Sea Tower|ja|日本海タワー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Sea Tower|1=Japan Sea Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508476}}
* {{ill|Niigata University|en|Niigata University|WD=Q267111|lt=Niigata University}} Medical School
* {{ill|Niigata Seiryo University|en|Niigata Seiryo University|WD=Q7034394|lt=Niigata Seiryo University}}
* {{ill|Niigata Seiryo University Junior College|ja|新潟青陵大学短期大学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Seiryo University Junior College|1=Niigata Seiryo WOMEN'S Junior College|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503612}}
* {{ill|Niigata Seiryo High School|ja|新潟青陵高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Seiryo High School|1=Niigata Seiryo High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503613}}
* {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Niigata Chuo High School|ja|新潟県立新潟中央高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Niigata Chuo High School|1=Niigata Prefectural Niigata Central High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503070}}
* {{ill|Niigata City Yorio Junior High School|ja|新潟市立寄居中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata City Yorio Junior High School|1=Niigata Shiritsu Yorii Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502909}}
* {{ill|Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Junior High School|ja|新潟大学教育学部附属新潟中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Junior High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟大学附属新潟中学校|1=Niigata Junior High School attached to Niigata University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502863}}
* {{ill|Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Elementary School|ja|新潟大学教育学部附属新潟小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟大学附属新潟小学校|1=Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502865}}
* {{ill|Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Special Support School|ja|新潟大学教育学部附属特別支援学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Special Support School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟大学附属特別支援学校|1=Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Special Support School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502864}}

'''Niigata Gokoku Shrine''' (新潟縣護國神社, Niigata-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}}) located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Niigata|en|Chūō-ku, Niigata|WD=Q711787|lt=Chūō-ku, Niigata}} in {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|WD=Q179691|lt=Niigata, Niigata}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}}.

The shrine enshrines war dead from Niigata Prefecture from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}} to the {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=Second World War}}, and currently houses 79,729 deities.

== History ==
The {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}, which started with the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}} and eventually spread nationwide, also turned Niigata City into a fierce battlefield. Many lives were lost on both sides: the Western Army (new government forces from {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain}}, {{ill|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|ja|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshū Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q876267}}, etc.) and the Eastern Army ({{ill|Yonezawa Domain|en|Yonezawa Domain|WD=Q1037948|lt=Yonezawa Domain}}, {{ill|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|ja|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389944}}, {{ill|Shōnai Domain|en|Shōnai Domain|WD=Q847303|lt=Shōnai Domain}}). In October 1868 (Meiji 1), a monument for the Western Army war dead was established in Tokiwagaoka (former site of {{ill|Niigata University|en|Niigata University|WD=Q267111|lt=Niigata University}} headquarters), and the shrine was built to enshrine 416 deities of the Boshin War's government forces as Niigata Shōkonsha. In 1875 (Meiji 8), it was renamed to Kankai Shōkonsha{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In July 1941 (Shōwa 16), it was renamed to Gokoku Shrine by Ministry of Home Affairs ordinance. There was intense competition to host the shrine among Niigata City, {{ill|Shibata, Niigata|en|Shibata, Niigata|WD=Q601950|lt=Shibata, Niigata}}, and {{ill|Takada, Niigata|ja|高田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada, Niigata|1=Takada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672106}}. In 1941 (Shōwa 16), Niigata City was chosen{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

From June 1942 (Shōwa 17), relocation to Nishifunamimachi began, and by May 1945 (Shōwa 20), the relocation was completed with the mobilization of 25,000 people{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In 1985 (Shōwa 60), 92 sets of remains believed to be from the Eastern Army war dead were discovered at the former Niigata University headquarters site, and they were reburied in the "Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery" within the Gokoku Shrine grounds. In 1987 (Shōwa 62), the "Boshin War Eastern Army Memorial" was erected.

* Niigata City Sumo Arena (now closed)

; Access

* Immediately from "Gokoku Shrine" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu C2 Hamaura Line (towards Niigata)
* Parking available

In the vicinity, there are several tourist attractions including aquariums, many schools, and the Japan Sea is close by. The coast near the shrine is selected as one of Japan's "100 best white sand and green pine beaches"{{sfn|林野庁|2019}}.

'''Niigata Gokoku Shrine''' (新潟縣護國神社, Niigata-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}}) located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Niigata|en|Chūō-ku, Niigata|WD=Q711787|lt=Chūō-ku, Niigata}} in {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|WD=Q179691|lt=Niigata, Niigata}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}}.

The shrine enshrines war dead from Niigata Prefecture from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}} to the {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=Second World War}}, and currently houses 79,729 deities.

== History ==
The {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}, which started with the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}} and eventually spread nationwide, also turned Niigata City into a fierce battlefield. Many lives were lost on both sides: the Western Army (new government forces from {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain}}, {{ill|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|ja|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshū Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q876267}}, etc.) and the Eastern Army ({{ill|Yonezawa Domain|en|Yonezawa Domain|WD=Q1037948|lt=Yonezawa Domain}}, {{ill|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|ja|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389944}}, {{ill|Shōnai Domain|en|Shōnai Domain|WD=Q847303|lt=Shōnai Domain}}). In October 1868 (Meiji 1), a monument for the Western Army war dead was established in Tokiwagaoka (former site of {{ill|Niigata University|en|Niigata University|WD=Q267111|lt=Niigata University}} headquarters), and the shrine was built to enshrine 416 deities of the Boshin War's government forces as Niigata Shōkonsha. In 1875 (Meiji 8), it was renamed to Kankai Shōkonsha{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In July 1941 (Shōwa 16), it was renamed to Gokoku Shrine by Ministry of Home Affairs ordinance. There was intense competition to host the shrine among Niigata City, {{ill|Shibata, Niigata|en|Shibata, Niigata|WD=Q601950|lt=Shibata, Niigata}}, and {{ill|Takada, Niigata|ja|高田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada, Niigata|1=Takada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672106}}. In 1941 (Shōwa 16), Niigata City was chosen{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

From June 1942 (Shōwa 17), relocation to Nishifunamimachi began, and by May 1945 (Shōwa 20), the relocation was completed with the mobilization of 25,000 people{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In 1985 (Shōwa 60), 92 sets of remains believed to be from the Eastern Army war dead were discovered at the former Niigata University headquarters site, and they were reburied in the "Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery" within the Gokoku Shrine grounds. In 1987 (Shōwa 62), the "Boshin War Eastern Army Memorial" was erected.

[[File:Boshin-eki Junnansha-cemetery.jpg|200px|thumb|Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery (Entrance)]]
[[File:Niigatagokokujinjya Syamon.jpg|200px|thumb|Myanmar White Marble Shrine Crest]]

== Grounds and Facilities ==
* Niigata City Sumo Arena (now closed)
* Basho Hall
* Sakaguchi Ango Memorial
* Kita Yoshitaka Monument (Monument of the song "Sunayama")
* Monument for the Victims of Manchurian and Mongolian Pioneers{{sfn|小林朗|2004}}
* Meiji University War Dead Memorial Hall

== Visitor Information ==
; Access
* From "Gokoku Shrine Entrance" bus stop on the {{ill|Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|ja|新潟市の交通#観光循環バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|1=transport in Niigata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16485562}}, a short walk
* About 5 minutes on foot from "Okamoto Shoji" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu {{ill|C2|ja|新潟交通のバス路線一覧#C2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/C2|1=C2|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502828}} Hamaura Line (from Niigata Station)
* Immediately from "Gokoku Shrine" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu C2 Hamaura Line (towards Niigata)
* Parking available

; Nearby
In the vicinity, there are several tourist attractions including aquariums, many schools, and the Japan Sea is close by. The coast near the shrine is selected as one of Japan's "100 best white sand and green pine beaches"{{sfn|林野庁|2019}}.
* {{ill|Marinepia Nihonkai|ja|マリンピア日本海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marinepia Nihonkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟市水族館 マリンピア日本海|1=Niigata City Aquarium Marinepia Nihonkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502898}}
* {{ill|Nishikaigan Park|ja|西海岸公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikaigan Park|1=West Coast Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629080}}
* {{ill|Japan Sea Tower|ja|日本海タワー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Sea Tower|1=Japan Sea Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508476}}
* {{ill|Niigata University|en|Niigata University|WD=Q267111|lt=Niigata University}} Medical School
* {{ill|Niigata Seiryo University|en|Niigata Seiryo University|WD=Q7034394|lt=Niigata Seiryo University}}
* {{ill|Niigata Seiryo University Junior College|ja|新潟青陵大学短期大学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Seiryo University Junior College|1=Niigata Seiryo WOMEN'S Junior College|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503612}}
* {{ill|Niigata Seiryo High School|ja|新潟青陵高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Seiryo High School|1=Niigata Seiryo High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503613}}
* {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Niigata Chuo High School|ja|新潟県立新潟中央高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Niigata Chuo High School|1=Niigata Prefectural Niigata Central High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503070}}
* {{ill|Niigata City Yorio Junior High School|ja|新潟市立寄居中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata City Yorio Junior High School|1=Niigata Shiritsu Yorii Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502909}}
* {{ill|Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Junior High School|ja|新潟大学教育学部附属新潟中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Junior High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟大学附属新潟中学校|1=Niigata Junior High School attached to Niigata University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502863}}
* {{ill|Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Elementary School|ja|新潟大学教育学部附属新潟小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟大学附属新潟小学校|1=Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502865}}
* {{ill|Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Special Support School|ja|新潟大学教育学部附属特別支援学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Special Support School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟大学附属特別支援学校|1=Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Special Support School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502864}}

'''Niigata Gokoku Shrine''' (新潟縣護國神社, Niigata-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}}) located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Niigata|en|Chūō-ku, Niigata|WD=Q711787|lt=Chūō-ku, Niigata}} in {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|WD=Q179691|lt=Niigata, Niigata}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture}}.

The shrine enshrines war dead from Niigata Prefecture from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}} to the {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=Second World War}}, and currently houses 79,729 deities.

== History ==
The {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}, which started with the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}} and eventually spread nationwide, also turned Niigata City into a fierce battlefield. Many lives were lost on both sides: the Western Army (new government forces from {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain}}, {{ill|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|ja|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshū Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q876267}}, etc.) and the Eastern Army ({{ill|Yonezawa Domain|en|Yonezawa Domain|WD=Q1037948|lt=Yonezawa Domain}}, {{ill|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|ja|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389944}}, {{ill|Shōnai Domain|en|Shōnai Domain|WD=Q847303|lt=Shōnai Domain}}). In October 1868 (Meiji 1), a monument for the Western Army war dead was established in Tokiwagaoka (former site of {{ill|Niigata University|en|Niigata University|WD=Q267111|lt=Niigata University}} headquarters), and the shrine was built to enshrine 416 deities of the Boshin War's government forces as Niigata Shōkonsha. In 1875 (Meiji 8), it was renamed to Kankai Shōkonsha{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In July 1941 (Shōwa 16), it was renamed to Gokoku Shrine by Ministry of Home Affairs ordinance. There was intense competition to host the shrine among Niigata City, {{ill|Shibata, Niigata|en|Shibata, Niigata|WD=Q601950|lt=Shibata, Niigata}}, and {{ill|Takada, Niigata|ja|高田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada, Niigata|1=Takada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672106}}. In 1941 (Shōwa 16), Niigata City was chosen{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

From June 1942 (Shōwa 17), relocation to Nishifunamimachi began, and by May 1945 (Shōwa 20), the relocation was completed with the mobilization of 25,000 people{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In 1985 (Shōwa 60), 92 sets of remains believed to be from the Eastern Army war dead were discovered at the former Niigata University headquarters site, and they were reburied in the "Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery" within the Gokoku Shrine grounds. In 1987 (Shōwa 62), the "Boshin War Eastern Army Memorial" was erected.

[[File:Boshin-eki Junnansha-cemetery.jpg|200px|thumb|Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery (Entrance)]]
[[File:Niigatagokokujinjya Syamon.jpg|200px|thumb|Myanmar White Marble Shrine Crest]]

== Grounds and Facilities ==
* Niigata City Sumo Arena (now closed)
* Basho Hall
* Sakaguchi Ango Memorial
* Kita Yoshitaka Monument (Monument of the song "Sunayama")
* Monument for the Victims of Manchurian and Mongolian Pioneers{{sfn|小林朗|2004}}
* Meiji University War Dead Memorial Hall

== Visitor Information ==
; Access
* From "Gokoku Shrine Entrance" bus stop on the {{ill|Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|ja|新潟市の交通#観光循環バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|1=transport in Niigata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16485562}}, a short walk
* About 5 minutes on foot from "Okamoto Shoji" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu {{ill|C2|ja|新潟交通のバス路線一覧#C2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/C2|1=C2|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502828}} Hamaura Line (from Niigata Station)
* Immediately from "Gokoku Shrine" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu C2 Hamaura Line (towards Niigata)
* Parking available

; Nearby
In the vicinity, there are several tourist attractions including aquariums, many schools, and the Japan Sea is close by. The coast near the shrine is selected as one of Japan's "100 best white sand and green pine beaches"{{sfn|林野庁|2019}}.

'''Niigata Gokoku Shrine''' (新潟縣護國神社, Niigata-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}) located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Niigata|en|Chūō-ku, Niigata|WD=Q711787|lt=Chūō-ku, Niigata|1=Chūō-ku, Niigata}} in {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|WD=Q179691|lt=Niigata, Niigata|1=Niigata (city)}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}.

The shrine enshrines war dead from Niigata Prefecture from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}} to the {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=Second World War|1=World War II}}, and currently houses 79,729 deities.

== History ==
The {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}, which started with the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi|1=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}} and eventually spread nationwide, also turned Niigata City into a fierce battlefield. Many lives were lost on both sides: the Western Army (new government forces from {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain|1=Satsuma Domain}}, {{ill|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|ja|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshū Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q876267}}, etc.) and the Eastern Army ({{ill|Yonezawa Domain|en|Yonezawa Domain|WD=Q1037948|lt=Yonezawa Domain|1=Yonezawa Domain}}, {{ill|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|ja|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389944}}, {{ill|Shōnai Domain|en|Shōnai Domain|WD=Q847303|lt=Shōnai Domain|1=Shōnai Domain}}). In October 1868 (Meiji 1), a monument for the Western Army war dead was established in Tokiwagaoka (former site of {{ill|Niigata University|en|Niigata University|WD=Q267111|lt=Niigata University|1=Niigata University}} headquarters), and the shrine was built to enshrine 416 deities of the Boshin War's government forces as Niigata Shōkonsha. In 1875 (Meiji 8), it was renamed to Kankai Shōkonsha{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In July 1941 (Shōwa 16), it was renamed to Gokoku Shrine by Ministry of Home Affairs ordinance. There was intense competition to host the shrine among Niigata City, {{ill|Shibata, Niigata|en|Shibata, Niigata|WD=Q601950|lt=Shibata, Niigata|1=Shibata, Niigata}}, and {{ill|Takada, Niigata|ja|高田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada, Niigata|1=Takada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672106}}. In 1941 (Shōwa 16), Niigata City was chosen{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

From June 1942 (Shōwa 17), relocation to Nishifunamimachi began, and by May 1945 (Shōwa 20), the relocation was completed with the mobilization of 25,000 people{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In 1985 (Shōwa 60), 92 sets of remains believed to be from the Eastern Army war dead were discovered at the former Niigata University headquarters site, and they were reburied in the "Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery" within the Gokoku Shrine grounds. In 1987 (Shōwa 62), the "Boshin War Eastern Army Memorial" was erected.

[[File:Boshin-eki Junnansha-cemetery.jpg|200px|thumb|Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery (Entrance)]]
[[File:Niigatagokokujinjya Syamon.jpg|200px|thumb|Myanmar White Marble Shrine Crest]]

== Grounds and Facilities ==
* Niigata City Sumo Arena (now closed)
* Basho Hall
* Sakaguchi Ango Memorial
* Kita Yoshitaka Monument (Monument of the song "Sunayama")
* Monument for the Victims of Manchurian and Mongolian Pioneers{{sfn|小林朗|2004}}
* Meiji University War Dead Memorial Hall

== Visitor Information ==
; Access
* From "Gokoku Shrine Entrance" bus stop on the {{ill|Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|ja|新潟市の交通#観光循環バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|1=transport in Niigata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16485562}}, a short walk
* About 5 minutes on foot from "Okamoto Shoji" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu {{ill|C2|ja|新潟交通のバス路線一覧#C2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/C2|1=Q11502828|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502828}} Hamaura Line (from Niigata Station)
* Immediately from "Gokoku Shrine" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu C2 Hamaura Line (towards Niigata)
* Parking available

; Nearby
In the vicinity, there are several tourist attractions including aquariums, many schools, and the Japan Sea is close by. The coast near the shrine is selected as one of Japan's "100 best white sand and green pine beaches"{{sfn|林野庁|2019}}.

* {{ill|Niigata Seiryo University|en|Niigata Seiryo University|WD=Q7034394|lt=Niigata Seiryo University|1=Niigata Seiryo University}}
* {{ill|Niigata Seiryo University Junior College|ja|新潟青陵大学短期大学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Seiryo University Junior College|1=Niigata Seiryo WOMEN'S Junior College|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503612}}
* {{ill|Niigata Seiryo High School|ja|新潟青陵高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Seiryo High School|1=Niigata Seiryo High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503613}}

* {{ill|Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Junior High School|ja|新潟大学教育学部附属新潟中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Junior High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟大学附属新潟中学校|1=Niigata Junior High School attached to Niigata University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502863}}

'''Niigata Gokoku Shrine''' (新潟縣護國神社, Niigata-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}) located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Niigata|en|Chūō-ku, Niigata|WD=Q711787|lt=Chūō-ku, Niigata|1=Chūō-ku, Niigata}} in {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|WD=Q179691|lt=Niigata, Niigata|1=Niigata (city)}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}.

The shrine enshrines war dead from Niigata Prefecture from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}} to the {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=Second World War|1=World War II}}, and currently houses 79,729 deities.

== History ==
The {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}, which started with the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi|1=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}} and eventually spread nationwide, also turned Niigata City into a fierce battlefield. Many lives were lost on both sides: the Western Army (new government forces from {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain|1=Satsuma Domain}}, {{ill|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|ja|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshū Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q876267}}, etc.) and the Eastern Army ({{ill|Yonezawa Domain|en|Yonezawa Domain|WD=Q1037948|lt=Yonezawa Domain|1=Yonezawa Domain}}, {{ill|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|ja|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389944}}, {{ill|Shōnai Domain|en|Shōnai Domain|WD=Q847303|lt=Shōnai Domain|1=Shōnai Domain}}). In October 1868 (Meiji 1), a monument for the Western Army war dead was established in Tokiwagaoka (former site of {{ill|Niigata University|en|Niigata University|WD=Q267111|lt=Niigata University|1=Niigata University}} headquarters), and the shrine was built to enshrine 416 deities of the Boshin War's government forces as Niigata Shōkonsha. In 1875 (Meiji 8), it was renamed to Kankai Shōkonsha{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In July 1941 (Shōwa 16), it was renamed to Gokoku Shrine by Ministry of Home Affairs ordinance. There was intense competition to host the shrine among Niigata City, {{ill|Shibata, Niigata|en|Shibata, Niigata|WD=Q601950|lt=Shibata, Niigata|1=Shibata, Niigata}}, and {{ill|Takada, Niigata|ja|高田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada, Niigata|1=Takada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672106}}. In 1941 (Shōwa 16), Niigata City was chosen{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

From June 1942 (Shōwa 17), relocation to Nishifunamimachi began, and by May 1945 (Shōwa 20), the relocation was completed with the mobilization of 25,000 people{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In 1985 (Shōwa 60), 92 sets of remains believed to be from the Eastern Army war dead were discovered at the former Niigata University headquarters site, and they were reburied in the "Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery" within the Gokoku Shrine grounds. In 1987 (Shōwa 62), the "Boshin War Eastern Army Memorial" was erected.

* Immediately from "Gokoku Shrine" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu C2 Hamaura Line (towards Niigata)

'''Niigata Gokoku Shrine''' (新潟縣護國神社, Niigata-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}) located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Niigata|en|Chūō-ku, Niigata|WD=Q711787|lt=Chūō-ku, Niigata|1=Chūō-ku, Niigata}} in {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|WD=Q179691|lt=Niigata, Niigata|1=Niigata (city)}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}.

== History ==
The {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}, which started with the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi|1=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}} and eventually spread nationwide, also turned Niigata City into a fierce battlefield. Many lives were lost on both sides: the Western Army (new government forces from {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain|1=Satsuma Domain}}, {{ill|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|ja|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshū Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q876267}}, etc.) and the Eastern Army ({{ill|Yonezawa Domain|en|Yonezawa Domain|WD=Q1037948|lt=Yonezawa Domain|1=Yonezawa Domain}}, {{ill|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|ja|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389944}}, {{ill|Shōnai Domain|en|Shōnai Domain|WD=Q847303|lt=Shōnai Domain|1=Shōnai Domain}}). In October 1868 (Meiji 1), a monument for the Western Army war dead was established in Tokiwagaoka (former site of {{ill|Niigata University|en|Niigata University|WD=Q267111|lt=Niigata University|1=Niigata University}} headquarters), and the shrine was built to enshrine 416 deities of the Boshin War's government forces as Niigata Shōkonsha. In 1875 (Meiji 8), it was renamed to Kankai Shōkonsha{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In July 1941 (Shōwa 16), it was renamed to Gokoku Shrine by Ministry of Home Affairs ordinance. There was intense competition to host the shrine among Niigata City, {{ill|Shibata, Niigata|en|Shibata, Niigata|WD=Q601950|lt=Shibata, Niigata|1=Shibata, Niigata}}, and {{ill|Takada, Niigata|ja|高田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada, Niigata|1=Takada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672106}}. In 1941 (Shōwa 16), Niigata City was chosen{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

From June 1942 (Shōwa 17), relocation to Nishifunamimachi began, and by May 1945 (Shōwa 20), the relocation was completed with the mobilization of 25,000 people{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In 1985 (Shōwa 60), 92 sets of remains believed to be from the Eastern Army war dead were discovered at the former Niigata University headquarters site, and they were reburied in the "Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery" within the Gokoku Shrine grounds. In 1987 (Shōwa 62), the "Boshin War Eastern Army Memorial" was erected.

[[File:Boshin-eki Junnansha-cemetery.jpg|200px|thumb|Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery (Entrance)]]
[[File:Niigatagokokujinjya Syamon.jpg|200px|thumb|Myanmar White Marble Shrine Crest]]

== Grounds and Facilities ==
* Niigata City Sumo Arena (now closed)
* Basho Hall
* Sakaguchi Ango Memorial
* Kita Yoshitaka Monument (Monument of the song "Sunayama")
* Monument for the Victims of Manchurian and Mongolian Pioneers{{sfn|小林朗|2004}}
* Meiji University War Dead Memorial Hall

== Visitor Information ==
; Access
* From "Gokoku Shrine Entrance" bus stop on the {{ill|Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|ja|新潟市の交通#観光循環バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|1=transport in Niigata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16485562}}, a short walk
* About 5 minutes on foot from "Okamoto Shoji" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu {{ill|C2|ja|新潟交通のバス路線一覧#C2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/C2|1=Q11502828|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502828}} Hamaura Line (from Niigata Station)
* Immediately from "Gokoku Shrine" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu C2 Hamaura Line (towards Niigata)
* Parking available

; Nearby
In the vicinity, there are several tourist attractions including aquariums, many schools, and the Japan Sea is close by. The coast near the shrine is selected as one of Japan's "100 best white sand and green pine beaches"{{sfn|林野庁|2019}}.
* {{ill|Marinepia Nihonkai|ja|マリンピア日本海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marinepia Nihonkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟市水族館 マリンピア日本海|1=Niigata City Aquarium Marinepia Nihonkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502898}}
* {{ill|Nishikaigan Park|ja|西海岸公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikaigan Park|1=West Coast Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629080}}
* {{ill|Japan Sea Tower|ja|日本海タワー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Sea Tower|1=Q11508476|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508476}}
* {{ill|Niigata University|en|Niigata University|WD=Q267111|lt=Niigata University|1=Niigata University}} Medical School
* {{ill|Niigata Seiryo University|en|Niigata Seiryo University|WD=Q7034394|lt=Niigata Seiryo University|1=Niigata Seiryo University}}
* {{ill|Niigata Seiryo University Junior College|ja|新潟青陵大学短期大学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Seiryo University Junior College|1=Niigata Seiryo WOMEN'S Junior College|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503612}}
* {{ill|Niigata Seiryo High School|ja|新潟青陵高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Seiryo High School|1=Niigata Seiryo High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503613}}
* {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Niigata Chuo High School|ja|新潟県立新潟中央高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Niigata Chuo High School|1=Niigata Prefectural Niigata Central High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503070}}
* {{ill|Niigata City Yorio Junior High School|ja|新潟市立寄居中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata City Yorio Junior High School|1=Niigata Shiritsu Yorii Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502909}}
* {{ill|Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Junior High School|ja|新潟大学教育学部附属新潟中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Junior High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟大学附属新潟中学校|1=Niigata Junior High School attached to Niigata University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502863}}
* {{ill|Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Elementary School|ja|新潟大学教育学部附属新潟小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟大学附属新潟小学校|1=Q11502865|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502865}}
* {{ill|Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Special Support School|ja|新潟大学教育学部附属特別支援学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Special Support School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟大学附属特別支援学校|1=Q11502864|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502864}}

'''Niigata Gokoku Shrine''' (新潟縣護國神社, Niigata-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}) located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Niigata|en|Chūō-ku, Niigata|WD=Q711787|lt=Chūō-ku, Niigata|1=Chūō-ku, Niigata}} in {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|WD=Q179691|lt=Niigata, Niigata|1=Niigata (city)}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}.

The shrine enshrines war dead from Niigata Prefecture from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}} to the {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=Second World War|1=World War II}}, and currently houses 79,729 deities.

== History ==
The {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}, which started with the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi|1=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}} and eventually spread nationwide, also turned Niigata City into a fierce battlefield. Many lives were lost on both sides: the Western Army (new government forces from {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain|1=Satsuma Domain}}, {{ill|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|ja|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshū Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q876267}}, etc.) and the Eastern Army ({{ill|Yonezawa Domain|en|Yonezawa Domain|WD=Q1037948|lt=Yonezawa Domain|1=Yonezawa Domain}}, {{ill|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|ja|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389944}}, {{ill|Shōnai Domain|en|Shōnai Domain|WD=Q847303|lt=Shōnai Domain|1=Shōnai Domain}}). In October 1868 (Meiji 1), a monument for the Western Army war dead was established in Tokiwagaoka (former site of {{ill|Niigata University|en|Niigata University|WD=Q267111|lt=Niigata University|1=Niigata University}} headquarters), and the shrine was built to enshrine 416 deities of the Boshin War's government forces as Niigata Shōkonsha. In 1875 (Meiji 8), it was renamed to Kankai Shōkonsha{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In July 1941 (Shōwa 16), it was renamed to Gokoku Shrine by Ministry of Home Affairs ordinance. There was intense competition to host the shrine among Niigata City, {{ill|Shibata, Niigata|en|Shibata, Niigata|WD=Q601950|lt=Shibata, Niigata|1=Shibata, Niigata}}, and {{ill|Takada, Niigata|ja|高田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada, Niigata|1=Takada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672106}}. In 1941 (Shōwa 16), Niigata City was chosen{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

From June 1942 (Shōwa 17), relocation to Nishifunamimachi began, and by May 1945 (Shōwa 20), the relocation was completed with the mobilization of 25,000 people{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In 1985 (Shōwa 60), 92 sets of remains believed to be from the Eastern Army war dead were discovered at the former Niigata University headquarters site, and they were reburied in the "Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery" within the Gokoku Shrine grounds. In 1987 (Shōwa 62), the "Boshin War Eastern Army Memorial" was erected.

== Grounds and Facilities ==
* Niigata City Sumo Arena (now closed)
* Basho Hall
* Sakaguchi Ango Memorial
* Kita Yoshitaka Monument (Monument of the song "Sunayama")
* Monument for the Victims of Manchurian and Mongolian Pioneers{{sfn|小林朗|2004}}

== Visitor Information ==

* About 5 minutes on foot from "Okamoto Shoji" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu {{ill|C2|ja|新潟交通のバス路線一覧#C2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/C2|1=Q11502828|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502828}} Hamaura Line (from Niigata Station)
* Immediately from "Gokoku Shrine" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu C2 Hamaura Line (towards Niigata)
* Parking available

; Nearby
In the vicinity, there are several tourist attractions including aquariums, many schools, and the Japan Sea is close by. The coast near the shrine is selected as one of Japan's "100 best white sand and green pine beaches"{{sfn|林野庁|2019}}.

'''Niigata Gokoku Shrine''' (新潟縣護國神社, Niigata-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}) located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Niigata|en|Chūō-ku, Niigata|WD=Q711787|lt=Chūō-ku, Niigata|1=Chūō-ku, Niigata}} in {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|WD=Q179691|lt=Niigata, Niigata|1=Niigata (city)}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}.

The shrine enshrines war dead from Niigata Prefecture from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}} to the {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=Second World War|1=World War II}}, and currently houses 79,729 deities.

== History ==
The {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}, which started with the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi|1=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}} and eventually spread nationwide, also turned Niigata City into a fierce battlefield. Many lives were lost on both sides: the Western Army (new government forces from {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain|1=Satsuma Domain}}, {{ill|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|ja|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshū Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q876267}}, etc.) and the Eastern Army ({{ill|Yonezawa Domain|en|Yonezawa Domain|WD=Q1037948|lt=Yonezawa Domain|1=Yonezawa Domain}}, {{ill|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|ja|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389944}}, {{ill|Shōnai Domain|en|Shōnai Domain|WD=Q847303|lt=Shōnai Domain|1=Shōnai Domain}}). In October 1868 (Meiji 1), a monument for the Western Army war dead was established in Tokiwagaoka (former site of {{ill|Niigata University|en|Niigata University|WD=Q267111|lt=Niigata University|1=Niigata University}} headquarters), and the shrine was built to enshrine 416 deities of the Boshin War's government forces as Niigata Shōkonsha. In 1875 (Meiji 8), it was renamed to Kankai Shōkonsha{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In July 1941 (Shōwa 16), it was renamed to Gokoku Shrine by Ministry of Home Affairs ordinance. There was intense competition to host the shrine among Niigata City, {{ill|Shibata, Niigata|en|Shibata, Niigata|WD=Q601950|lt=Shibata, Niigata|1=Shibata, Niigata}}, and {{ill|Takada, Niigata|ja|高田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada, Niigata|1=Takada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672106}}. In 1941 (Shōwa 16), Niigata City was chosen{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

From June 1942 (Shōwa 17), relocation to Nishifunamimachi began, and by May 1945 (Shōwa 20), the relocation was completed with the mobilization of 25,000 people{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In 1985 (Shōwa 60), 92 sets of remains believed to be from the Eastern Army war dead were discovered at the former Niigata University headquarters site, and they were reburied in the "Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery" within the Gokoku Shrine grounds. In 1987 (Shōwa 62), the "Boshin War Eastern Army Memorial" was erected.

[[File:Boshin-eki Junnansha-cemetery.jpg|200px|thumb|Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery (Entrance)]]
[[File:Niigatagokokujinjya Syamon.jpg|200px|thumb|Myanmar White Marble Shrine Crest]]

== Grounds and Facilities ==
* Niigata City Sumo Arena (now closed)
* Basho Hall
* Sakaguchi Ango Memorial
* Kita Yoshitaka Monument (Monument of the song "Sunayama")
* Monument for the Victims of Manchurian and Mongolian Pioneers{{sfn|小林朗|2004}}
* Meiji University War Dead Memorial Hall

== Visitor Information ==
; Access
* From "Gokoku Shrine Entrance" bus stop on the {{ill|Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|ja|新潟市の交通#観光循環バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|1=transport in Niigata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16485562}}, a short walk
* About 5 minutes on foot from "Okamoto Shoji" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu {{ill|C2|ja|新潟交通のバス路線一覧#C2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/C2|1=Q11502828|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502828}} Hamaura Line (from Niigata Station)
* Immediately from "Gokoku Shrine" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu C2 Hamaura Line (towards Niigata)
* Parking available

; Nearby
In the vicinity, there are several tourist attractions including aquariums, many schools, and the Japan Sea is close by. The coast near the shrine is selected as one of Japan's "100 best white sand and green pine beaches"{{sfn|林野庁|2019}}.
* {{ill|Marinepia Nihonkai|ja|マリンピア日本海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Marinepia Nihonkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟市水族館 マリンピア日本海|1=Niigata City Aquarium Marinepia Nihonkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502898}}
* {{ill|Nishikaigan Park|ja|西海岸公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishikaigan Park|1=West Coast Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629080}}
* {{ill|Japan Sea Tower|ja|日本海タワー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Sea Tower|1=Q11508476|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508476}}
* {{ill|Niigata University|en|Niigata University|WD=Q267111|lt=Niigata University|1=Niigata University}} Medical School
* {{ill|Niigata Seiryo University|en|Niigata Seiryo University|WD=Q7034394|lt=Niigata Seiryo University|1=Niigata Seiryo University}}
* {{ill|Niigata Seiryo University Junior College|ja|新潟青陵大学短期大学部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Seiryo University Junior College|1=Niigata Seiryo WOMEN'S Junior College|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503612}}
* {{ill|Niigata Seiryo High School|ja|新潟青陵高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Seiryo High School|1=Niigata Seiryo High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503613}}
* {{ill|Niigata Prefectural Niigata Chuo High School|ja|新潟県立新潟中央高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata Prefectural Niigata Chuo High School|1=Niigata Prefectural Niigata Central High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503070}}
* {{ill|Niigata City Yorio Junior High School|ja|新潟市立寄居中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata City Yorio Junior High School|1=Niigata Shiritsu Yorii Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502909}}
* {{ill|Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Junior High School|ja|新潟大学教育学部附属新潟中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Junior High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟大学附属新潟中学校|1=Niigata Junior High School attached to Niigata University|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502863}}
* {{ill|Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Elementary School|ja|新潟大学教育学部附属新潟小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Niigata Elementary School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟大学附属新潟小学校|1=Q11502865|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502865}}
* {{ill|Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Special Support School|ja|新潟大学教育学部附属特別支援学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata University Faculty of Education Affiliated Special Support School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新潟大学附属特別支援学校|1=Q11502864|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502864}}
* Niigata Prefectural Hamagumi Special Support School

'''Niigata Gokoku Shrine''' (新潟縣護國神社, Niigata-ken Gokoku Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}) located in {{ill|Chūō-ku, Niigata|en|Chūō-ku, Niigata|WD=Q711787|lt=Chūō-ku, Niigata|1=Chūō-ku, Niigata}} in {{ill|Niigata (city)|en|Niigata (city)|WD=Q179691|lt=Niigata, Niigata|1=Niigata (city)}}, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}.

The shrine enshrines war dead from Niigata Prefecture from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}} to the {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=Second World War|1=World War II}}, and currently houses 79,729 deities.

== History ==
The {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}, which started with the {{ill|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|en|Battle of Toba–Fushimi|WD=Q1193959|lt=Battle of Toba–Fushimi|1=Battle of Toba–Fushimi}} and eventually spread nationwide, also turned Niigata City into a fierce battlefield. Many lives were lost on both sides: the Western Army (new government forces from {{ill|Satsuma Domain|en|Satsuma Domain|WD=Q676187|lt=Satsuma Domain|1=Satsuma Domain}}, {{ill|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|ja|Chōshū Domain|1=Chōshū Domain|2=ja|3=長州藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshū Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q876267}}, etc.) and the Eastern Army ({{ill|Yonezawa Domain|en|Yonezawa Domain|WD=Q1037948|lt=Yonezawa Domain|1=Yonezawa Domain}}, {{ill|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|ja|Aizu Domain|1=Aizu Domain|2=ja|3=会津藩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu Domain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4389944}}, {{ill|Shōnai Domain|en|Shōnai Domain|WD=Q847303|lt=Shōnai Domain|1=Shōnai Domain}}). In October 1868 (Meiji 1), a monument for the Western Army war dead was established in Tokiwagaoka (former site of {{ill|Niigata University|en|Niigata University|WD=Q267111|lt=Niigata University|1=Niigata University}} headquarters), and the shrine was built to enshrine 416 deities of the Boshin War's government forces as Niigata Shōkonsha. In 1875 (Meiji 8), it was renamed to Kankai Shōkonsha{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In July 1941 (Shōwa 16), it was renamed to Gokoku Shrine by Ministry of Home Affairs ordinance. There was intense competition to host the shrine among Niigata City, {{ill|Shibata, Niigata|en|Shibata, Niigata|WD=Q601950|lt=Shibata, Niigata|1=Shibata, Niigata}}, and {{ill|Takada, Niigata|ja|高田市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takada, Niigata|1=Takada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672106}}. In 1941 (Shōwa 16), Niigata City was chosen{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

From June 1942 (Shōwa 17), relocation to Nishifunamimachi began, and by May 1945 (Shōwa 20), the relocation was completed with the mobilization of 25,000 people{{sfn|新潟市|1996}}.

In 1985 (Shōwa 60), 92 sets of remains believed to be from the Eastern Army war dead were discovered at the former Niigata University headquarters site, and they were reburied in the "Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery" within the Gokoku Shrine grounds. In 1987 (Shōwa 62), the "Boshin War Eastern Army Memorial" was erected.

[[File:Boshin-eki Junnansha-cemetery.jpg|200px|thumb|Boshin War Martyrs Cemetery (Entrance)]]
[[File:Niigatagokokujinjya Syamon.jpg|200px|thumb|Myanmar White Marble Shrine Crest]]

== Grounds and Facilities ==
* Niigata City Sumo Arena (now closed)
* Basho Hall
* Sakaguchi Ango Memorial
* Kita Yoshitaka Monument (Monument of the song "Sunayama")
* Monument for the Victims of Manchurian and Mongolian Pioneers{{sfn|小林朗|2004}}
* Meiji University War Dead Memorial Hall

== Visitor Information ==
; Access
* From "Gokoku Shrine Entrance" bus stop on the {{ill|Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|ja|新潟市の交通#観光循環バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niigata City Tourist Circulation Bus|1=transport in Niigata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16485562}}, a short walk
* About 5 minutes on foot from "Okamoto Shoji" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu {{ill|C2|ja|新潟交通のバス路線一覧#C2|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/C2|1=Q11502828|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502828}} Hamaura Line (from Niigata Station)
* Immediately from "Gokoku Shrine" bus stop on the Niigata Kotsu C2 Hamaura Line (towards Niigata)
* Parking available

; Nearby
In the vicinity, there are several tourist attractions including aquariums, many schools, and the Japan Sea is close by. The coast near the shrine is selected as one of Japan's "100 best white sand and green pine beaches"{{sfn|林野庁|2019}}.
